diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: High-volume watery diarrhoea after a suspect meal is the core feature of infective gastroenteritis.
Obstruction produces absolute constipation, not profuse liquid stool.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent forceful vomiting with inability to retain oral fluids

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Vomiting accompanying diarrhoea is typical; its persistence is what makes oral rehydration fail.
Failure to retain any oral intake drives ongoing net fluid loss and is a marker of severity.
Diffuse colicky pain preceding stools fits enteritis.
Generalised non-localised pain lowers the probability, but localisation can evolve — an abdominal exam is required to exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Postural light-headedness / presyncope on standing (room goes black)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Patient-reportable proxy for orthostatic hypotension — a marker of significant intravascular depletion.
Systemic features accompany infective gastroenteritis, particularly bacterial/foodborne causes.

symptoms.symptom_name: Ingestion of takeaway chicken ~24 hours before onset

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A plausible foodborne exposure (e.g. Campylobacter from poultry) with a compatible incubation window.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of blood or melaena in stool/vomit

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No current bleeding features; a change here would escalate concern and triage.
Objective postural drop with tachycardia — sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers volume depletion from the reported presyncope, not a measured BP.
Compensatory tachycardia supports significant fluid loss; the measured heart rate is clinician-obtained (patient may report a racing heart).

symptoms.symptom_name: Dry mucous membranes and poor skin turgor on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic clinical signs of dehydration — a clinician assessment sealed from the AI-readable side.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft, non-distended abdomen with hyperactive bowel sounds and NO guarding, rigidity or rebound

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A reassuring non-peritonitic abdomen with hyperactive borborygmi is the finding that excludes the surgical mimic — and is exactly what telehealth cannot obtain.
Hyperactive borborygmi with diffuse mild tenderness fits enteritis rather than obstruction or peritonitis.

investigation_details.expected_result: Possible bacterial enteric pathogen (e.g. Campylobacter) — pending; only indicated if symptoms persist beyond 72 hours
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Acceptable to omit early; delays pathogen identification only if the illness fails to settle.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms and characterises the pathogen retrospectively; not required to make the working diagnosis or to triage.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together — persistent vomiting with failed oral intake, orthostatic presyncope, postural hypotension, tachycardia and dry mucosae — these define significant volume depletion and set the triage tier.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person assessment with abdominal examination and clinician orthostatic vital signs
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Same-day face-to-face assessment is required to exclude an acute surgical abdomen and to formally quantify volume depletion — neither is achievable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Venous bloods — urea, electrolytes and renal function
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To detect electrolyte disturbance and acute kidney injury from dehydration; obtained at the in-person/ED assessment.
Only if symptoms persist beyond 72 hours or become bloody — does not change acute management or triage.
No remote indication; imaging is decided at in-person assessment only if the examination raises concern for a surgical abdomen. The AI must not over-order it.
